clusters.title: Can the second coming of Tony Abbott resurrect the Liberal party? Or is it another step towards ‘self-destruction’
clusters.neutral_headline: Tony Abbott elected unopposed as Liberal Party president
bullet_summary: “The door must be shut,” Taylor said of his approach to prospective migrants who didn’t subscribe to Australia’s “core beliefs”
Reported by Guardian Australia, ABC News Australia

items.title: Can the second coming of Tony Abbott resurrect the Liberal party? Or is it another step towards ‘self-destruction’
items.standfirst: Angus Taylor believes the former PM is uniquely placed to help the party as its new president, but some fear he will render it even more unelectableGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastImmediately after ousting Sussan Ley, Angus Taylor used his first press conference as opposition leader to debut a slogan he hoped would define a new era of Liberal conservatism.“The door must be shut,” Taylor said of his approach to prospective migrants who didn’t subscribe to Australia’s “core beliefs”. Continue reading...
items.content: Angus Taylor believes the former PM is uniquely placed to help the party as its new president, but some fear he will render it even more unelectableGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastImmediately after ousting Sussan Ley, Angus Taylor used his first press conference as opposition leader to debut a slogan he hoped would define a new era of Liberal conservatism.“The door must be shut,” Taylor said of his approach to prospective migrants who didn’t subscribe to Australia’s “core beliefs”. Continue reading...

items.title: Tony Abbott says Liberals in ‘existential crisis’ as he is elected party president – as it happened

items.title: Abbott says Liberals ‘under new management’ and vows to help Taylor lead ‘people’s revolt’ against Labor
items.standfirst: Former prime minister elected Liberal party president on Friday after running unopposedFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastTony Abbott has declared the Liberals are “under new management” after his election as party president, vowing to help Angus Taylor lead a “people’s revolt” against the Labor government.The former prime minister and conservative warrior was elected to the position at the Liberal federal council meeting in Melbourne on Friday, marking a return to active political life seven years after losing his seat in federal parliament. Continue reading...
items.content: Former prime minister elected Liberal party president on Friday after running unopposedFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastTony Abbott has declared the Liberals are “under new management” after his election as party president, vowing to help Angus Taylor lead a “people’s revolt” against the Labor government.The former prime minister and conservative warrior was elected to the position at the Liberal federal council meeting in Melbourne on Friday, marking a return to active political life seven years after losing his seat in federal parliament. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?
clusters.neutral_headline: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister

items.title: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?
items.standfirst: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister? You might have seen AI generated memes of Albanese on social media following the announcement of the federal budget. Whether or not those are legal comes down to what the post is actually communicating to the people seeing it online.
items.content: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister? You might have seen AI generated memes of Albanese on social media following the announcement of the federal budget. Whether or not those are legal comes down to what the post is actually communicating to the people seeing it online.
